scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} of 2024

BETWEEN:

RAM KUMAR,
S/o {{FATHER_NAME}},
Age: 45 years,
Occupation: {{OCCUPATION}},
Resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka, {{PIN_CODE}}.

... PLAINTIFF

AND:

SURESH PATEL,
S/o {{FATHER_NAME}},
Age: 38 years,
Occupation: {{OCCUPATION}},
Resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, Karnataka, {{PIN_CODE}}.

... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

FACTS

1. The Plaintiff is a resident of Bengaluru and the Defendant is also a resident of Bengaluru. The parties are known to each other.

2. On 15.03.2024, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff requesting financial assistance as a hand loan. Acting on the request and trust, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant.

3. The said amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- was transferred via NEFT bank transfer on 15.03.2024. The unique transaction reference number (UTR) for the transfer is AXIB20240315123456.

4. There was no written agreement executed between the parties regarding the repayment terms. However, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount and the debt liability via WhatsApp messages exchanged between the parties.

5. The parties orally understood that the loan was repayable on demand. The Defendant failed to repay the amount despite the Plaintiff's repeated oral demands.

6. For the purpose of interest computation, the date of default is 20.06.2024. The Plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024.

7. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to repay the outstanding amount. The Defendant has failed to comply.

8. Despite the demands and the legal notice, the Defendant has failed to repay the principal amount or the accrued interest.

9. The total outstanding as on the date of filing comprises the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- along with interest at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024.

10. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the said amounts from the Defendant.

LEGAL BASIS

11. The suit is filed based on the Defendant’s breach of contractual obligation to repay the money lent.

12. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

13. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for territorial jurisdiction.

14. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for interest.

15. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 35 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for costs.

16. The suit is within limitation under Article 21 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action first arose on 15.03.2024 at Bengaluru when the Plaintiff advanced the hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant and the Defendant became liable to repay the same.

18. The cause of action further arose on/after 20.06.2024 when, upon the Plaintiff’s demand for repayment, the Defendant failed and neglected to repay, giving rise to the Plaintiff’s right to sue.

19. The legal notice dated 01.07.2024 and the Defendant’s continued non-payment thereafter form part of the continuing cause of action. The cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court as the Defendant resides in Bengaluru and the transaction took place in Bengaluru.

LIMITATION

20. The suit is governed by Article 21 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

21. The period of limitation is three years, commencing from 15.03.2024 (the date of the loan).

22. The present suit is filed within the prescribed period of limitation and is therefore maintainable.

JURISDICTION

23. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 as the Defendant resides within the jurisdiction of this Court at Koramangala, Bengaluru.

24. The cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Court as the loan was advanced and repayment was demanded in Bengaluru.

25. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the suit as the value of the claim is within the pecuniary limits of this Court.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

26. The suit is valued for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee at Rs. {{TOTAL_CLAIM_AMOUNT}}, comprising:
   (i) Principal amount: Rs. 15,00,000/-; and
   (ii) Pre-suit interest at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 to {{DATE_OF_FILING}}: Rs. {{PRE_SUIT_INTEREST_AMOUNT}}.

27. The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid/payable on the plaint.

INTEREST

28. The Plaintiff claims pre-suit interest at the rate of 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 till the date of filing of the suit.

29. The Plaintiff claims pendente lite interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

30. The Plaintiff claims future interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 from the date of decree till realisation.

SCHEDULE OF PROPERTY

31. Not Applicable. This is a suit for recovery of money and does not involve immovable property.

PRAYER

The Plaintiff respectfully prays for:

(a)
